era_context: Continuation of the late 5th Dynasty in Egypt, the Akkadian Empire's territorial dominance in Mesopotamia, and the mature Harappan phase of the Indus Valley Civilization. The Longshan culture continues in China's Yellow River region. This is a period of high urban civilization in the Old World's three major centers (Egypt, Mesopotamia, Indus Valley), though political fragmentation is beginning in Egypt.

events.title: Continued reign of Unas or early 6th Dynasty transition
events.description: This year falls within the reign of Unas or the very beginning of the 6th Dynasty (c. 2345 BCE onward). The transition between these dynasties is poorly documented; it may have been peaceful or involved succession disputes. The 6th Dynasty marks a shift toward greater decentralization of power, with provincial governors gaining more autonomy. Pyramid construction continues, though on a smaller scale than earlier in the Old Kingdom.
events.certainty_note: The exact year of the 5th–6th Dynasty transition is debated; conventional dating places it c. 2345 BCE, but uncertainty is ±10–20 years.

disconfirming_evidence: No specific events are documented for this year. The historical record for -2688 is essentially blank; any narrative is speculative based on broader era context.
historiographic_note: This year is not documented in any surviving historical source. The narrative above is inferred from broader chronological frameworks and archaeological context. The reliability of assigning specific events to this year is extremely low.
